# VER-QUIC-API-0005 - Incursa.Quic Pending Outbound Stream Open Verification

## Scope

This artifact covers the pending outbound open slice only: a blocked outbound open remains incomplete when the peer limit is exhausted, completes after later real `MAX_STREAMS` growth makes the requested direction available, and still honors cancellation while pending. It does not claim `STREAMS_BLOCKED` emission, broader stream-management parity, `0-RTT`, key update, or retry changes.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-API-0014

## Verification Method

Requirement-home execution, inspection, and trace render/check for the pending outbound open slice on the supported active loopback path.

## Preconditions

- The supported active loopback path and later real `MAX_STREAMS` growth path already exist.
- The runtime can keep a blocked outbound open request queued instead of failing fast.
- The existing cancellation and terminal-state semantics remain owned by `REQ-QUIC-API-0008`.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run `tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/QUIC/REQ-QUIC-API-0014.cs` to prove blocked pending open behavior, later real `MAX_STREAMS` wake-up, and blocked-open cancellation.
- Run the existing `REQ-QUIC-API-0008` cancellation coverage and `REQ-QUIC-API-0009` stream-capacity coverage to keep the adjacent open-stream and capacity slices green.
- Run `git diff --check`.
- Run the repo-local SpecTrace render/check flow for the changed canonical JSON artifacts and confirm the generated markdown views are current.

## Expected Result

The requirement-home proof shows a blocked outbound open staying pending until later real `MAX_STREAMS` growth resumes it, the request cancellation path still settles a blocked pending open, and the trace/doc artifacts stay aligned with the new requirement ID and supporting architecture/work-item records.

## Status

Landed; this verification slice records the proof burden for keeping blocked outbound opens pending until later real `MAX_STREAMS` growth resumes them without widening the public stream contract.
